_doc: Quiz-poll engagement bait for the Telegram channel. Each drives to an article via the explanation. Runner posts the next un-posted one.

polls.q: How many contractors does Angi sell each lead to?
polls.explain: 5–12 — which is why your real cost per booked job runs $1,400+. booked-job.com/blog/is-angi-worth-it/

polls.q: A 50% markup equals what PROFIT MARGIN?
polls.explain: 33% — markup ≠ margin. Most shops underprice here. booked-job.com/blog/how-to-price-a-job/

polls.q: What % of contractor invoices go unpaid past 30 days?
polls.explain: ~30% — a same-day follow-up text fixes most of it. booked-job.com

polls.q: Best time to ask for a Google review?
polls.explain: Peak gratitude — ask when the job's done, then text the link. booked-job.com/blog/get-more-google-reviews/

polls.q: In the slow season, smart shops...
polls.explain: Increase it — competitors go quiet, ad costs drop. booked-job.com/blog/surviving-the-slow-season/

polls.q: What % of people leave a contractor website without ever calling?
polls.explain: ~98% — usually because the phone number isn't a giant tap-to-call button. booked-job.com/blog/contractor-website-one-job/

polls.q: How fast should you respond to a new lead to actually win it?
polls.explain: ~5 minutes. The first to respond usually wins — not the best, the fastest. booked-job.com/blog/speed-to-lead-for-contractors/

polls.q: How many free spots are in Google's local 'map pack'?
polls.explain: 3 free spots above everything else. Your Google Business Profile is the ticket in. booked-job.com/blog/google-business-profile-for-contractors/

polls.q: What's the #1 thing customers judge before they ever call you?
polls.explain: Your reviews — count, recency, and how you reply. 140 recent beats 12, even at a higher price. booked-job.com/blog/why-reviews-win-the-call/

polls.q: Which usually costs you LESS per booked job?
polls.explain: Exclusive leads. The cheap shared lead is a footrace — the expensive one is often the cheaper job. booked-job.com/blog/should-you-pay-for-leads/

polls.q: Roughly how many people now ask an AI tool (not Google) who to hire?
polls.explain: About a third — and the same foundation gets you found in both. booked-job.com/blog/get-found-by-google-and-ai/
